zh.lifeSummary: James Audley 2nd Baron Audley於1312年1月3日出生在英國諾廷加姆爾聶薩爾。他於1330年6月13日之前與Joan de Mortimer在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1386年4月1日在英國斯塔福爾海利享年74歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾斯塔福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: James Audley 2nd Baron Audley于1312年1月3日出生在英国諾廷加姆爾聶薩爾。他于1330年6月13日之前与Joan de Mortimer在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1386年4月1日在英国斯塔福爾海利享年74岁，埋葬在英国英格兰斯塔福爾斯塔福德。

nameMeanings.name: Audley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Audley in Staffordshire, named from the Old English female personal name Aldgȳth + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: James Audley 2nd Baron Audley was born on 3 January 1312, in Kneesall, Nottinghamshire, England. He married Joan de Mortimer before 13 June 1330, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 1 April 1386, in Heighley, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 74, and was buried in Stafford, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: James Audley 2nd Baron Audley est né le 3 janvier 1312, à Kneesall, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre. Il a épousé Joan de Mortimer avant 13 juin 1330, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 1 avril 1386, à Heighley, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Stafford, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: James Audley 2nd Baron Audley wurde am 3. Januar 1312 in Kneesall, Nottinghamshire, England geboren. Er hat vor 13. Juni 1330 in England Joan de Mortimer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 1. April 1386 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Heighley, Staffordshire, England und wurde in Stafford, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: James Audley 2nd Baron Audley è nato il il 3 gennaio 1312 nel Kneesall, Nottinghamshire, England. Lui sposato Joan de Mortimer prima del 13 giugno 1330, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 1 aprile 1386, nel Heighley, Staffordshire, England, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolto a Stafford, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: James Audley 2nd Baron Audleyは1312年1月3日にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアネエサルで生まれた。1330年6月13日以前にイギリスでJoan de Mortimerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1386年4月1日にイギリススタフォードシュアイアヘイグhリーで74歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアスタフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James Audley 2nd Baron Audley(은)는 1312년 1월 3일 영국 노틴그함실르 니사에서 태어났다. 그는 1330년 6월 13일 이전 영국에서 Joan de Mortimer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1386년 4월 1일 영국 스태퍼드실르 헤이리에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.3 January 1312–1 April 1386: 3 de janeiro de 1312–1 de abril de 1386
pt.lifeSummary: James Audley 2nd Baron Audley nasceu em 3 de janeiro de 1312, em Kneesall, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Joan de Mortimer antes 13 de junho de 1330, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1 de abril de 1386, em Heighley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, com 74 anos, e foi sepultado em Stafford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: James Audley 2nd Baron Audley родился 3 января 1312 г. в местности нисалл, ноттингхамшир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Mortimer перед 13 июнь 1330 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 1 апреля 1386 г. в местности хигли, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности стаффорд, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.3 January 1312–1 April 1386: 3 de enero de 1312–1 de abril de 1386
es.lifeSummary: James Audley 2nd Baron Audley nació el 3 de enero de 1312, en Kneesall, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Joan de Mortimer antes de 13 de junio de 1330, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 1 de abril de 1386, en Heighley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultado en Stafford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
